2026-05.executive_summary: May maintenance was completed successfully — all plugins are current, PHP has been upgraded to 8.2, and the site remained available 100% of the time. However, website performance on mobile devices is a significant concern. The site scores 26/100 on Google's mobile speed test, which is well below the threshold where search rankings and visitor experience are affected. The root cause is heavy JavaScript loading before the page becomes usable. A performance remediation plan is the recommended next step.
highlights: No security threats detected — WAF active
key_risks: Mobile PageSpeed score of 26/100 — well below the 60 minimum threshold
970ms Total Blocking Time is preventing interactive use for nearly 1 second
Bootstrap and FontAwesome contributing 47KB of render-blocking CSS

performance.headline: Mobile performance requires urgent attention
performance.interpretation: Barnardos Australia scores 26/100 on Google's mobile page speed test, run live on 7 May 2026. The main cause is 970ms of Total Blocking Time — JavaScript resources are preventing the page from becoming interactive for nearly a full second. The primary offenders are Bootstrap, FontAwesome, jQuery, and a Convert Experiments analytics script that adds 780ms on its own. Together these resources account for over 15 seconds of network loading time.

metrics.label: PageSpeed (Mobile)
metrics.description: Significantly below the acceptable minimum of 60

metrics.label: Total Blocking Time
metrics.description: Heavy JavaScript blocking interactivity for nearly 1 second

metrics.label: Largest Contentful Paint
metrics.description: Just over the 2.5s good threshold — improvable

metrics.label: Layout Stability (CLS)
metrics.description: Content shifts slightly while loading — just over 0.1

metrics.label: First Contentful Paint
metrics.description: First content appears quickly — server response is fast

metrics.label: Speed Index
metrics.description: Page takes too long to appear visually complete

performance.risk: A PageSpeed score of 26 is a confirmed Google ranking penalty. Barnardos' donation and service pages are likely losing organic search traffic. Visitors on slow mobile connections will abandon the site before it becomes usable.
performance.recommended_action: Conduct a performance remediation sprint: defer the Convert Experiments script, lazy-load FontAwesome, and audit render-blocking CSS. Expected outcome: +20–30 PageSpeed points.

security.headline: No threats detected — all security systems active
security.interpretation: The WAF (Web Application Firewall) is active and blocking malicious traffic. No malware, no suspicious logins, and no known vulnerabilities in installed plugins. SSL certificate is valid with 280 days remaining.

metrics.label: WAF Status
metrics.description: Firewall blocking threats at the edge

metrics.description: No malware or suspicious files detected

metrics.label: SSL Certificate
metrics.description: Certificate valid and auto-renewing

metrics.label: Login Security
metrics.description: No brute-force attempts detected this month

security.risk: No current security risk. Continued WAF monitoring and plugin update discipline are the primary controls.

reliability.headline: 100% uptime — site fully available all month
reliability.interpretation: The site was available 100% of the time over the past 30 days with no downtime events recorded. WP Engine's infrastructure handled all traffic without incident.

updates.headline: All plugins and themes are current
updates.interpretation: All 0 pending updates were applied during the May maintenance window. WordPress core, all plugins, and the active theme are running current versions. PHP has been upgraded to 8.2.

backups.interpretation: Automated daily backups are completing without error on WP Engine. The most recent backup was verified and is restorable. Retention policy covers the last 30 days.

engineering.headline: Clean deployment history — 2 successful deployments
engineering.interpretation: Two deployments were made to production this month as part of the M&S maintenance work: the PHP 8.2 upgrade and the plugin update batch. Both completed without rollback or incident.

metrics.label: Hotfixes
metrics.description: No emergency fixes required

engineering.risk: No engineering risk. Clean deployment history indicates well-managed change control.

recommended_actions.title: Defer Convert Experiments analytics script and lazy-load FontAwesome icons
recommended_actions.impact: These two changes account for ~1,560ms of blocking time. Expected improvement: +15–20 PageSpeed points. Google uses mobile speed as a ranking factor — this directly impacts organic search traffic to Barnardos' donation and service pages.

recommended_actions.title: Audit and reduce render-blocking CSS — Bootstrap and FontAwesome total 47KB of blocking CSS
recommended_actions.impact: Reducing render-blocking CSS improves Speed Index and LCP, making the page feel significantly faster. Replace with utility-only classes where possible.

recommended_actions.title: Enable WP Engine CDN caching and configure static asset cache headers
recommended_actions.impact: Low-effort infrastructure change with meaningful impact on repeat visitor load times. Can be done without code changes.

recommended_actions.title: Remove jQuery Migrate (5.2KB) — no legacy jQuery code should require it on a current build
recommended_actions.impact: Reduces page weight and eliminates an unnecessary legacy dependency from every page load.
